 {"id":"bd-mjh3.16","title":"[FRX-16] Executable Graveyard and Repro/Legal Provenance Gate","description":"Make alien-method adoption executable, reproducible, and legally/provenance-safe.

Scope:
- adopted-primitive metadata schema and verification checklist,
- reproducibility/legal/provenance bundle generation,
- demo-to-claim linkage for production-facing milestones.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every adopted advanced primitive has structured metadata, verification status, and risk notes.
2. Reproducibility/legal/provenance bundles are generated and validated automatically.
3. Production-facing claims are linked to runnable demos and evidence IDs.

Program-Wide Test Gate:
- Every child task must define comprehensive unit-test scope, end-to-end scenario scripts, and detailed structured logging artifacts.
- Feature closure requires linked evidence that unit/e2e suites passed with deterministic fixtures and replay/triage logs.
- Any missing or stale test/logging evidence blocks milestone promotion and release gating.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-25T02:39:23.758790042Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:54.457068918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","extreme-optimization","frankenreact-sidecar","lane-governance","phase-frontier","react-compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.16","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.16.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:38.435622449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.16","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.16.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:38.535527165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.16","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.16.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:38.636938107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.16.1","title":"[FRX-16.1] Primitive Adoption Schema + Verification Checklist + Reuse Scan Gate","description":"Create primitive-adoption schema with mandatory verification fields.

Include:
- primary-paper verification checklist status,
- EV/relevance/risk scoring,
- fallback trigger and budgeted mode summary,
- crate reuse/adopt-vs-build rationale.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Adoption schema is machine-readable and required for every advanced primitive in FRX.
2. Missing verification or fallback metadata blocks primitive activation.
3. Crate-reuse scan outcome is recorded before S/A-tier implementation.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHeron","created_at":"2026-02-25T02:39:24.273276143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:14:09.571588902Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:14:09.571560549Z","close_reason":"Implemented schema+docs+tests+gate artifacts; focused check/test pass via rch; remaining clippy failure is external structural_causal_model drift","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","extreme-optimization","frankenreact-sidecar","lane-governance","phase-frontier","react-compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.16.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:36:29.767887537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":275,"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.16.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FRX-16.1 implementation complete in reserved scope:\n- crates/franken-engine/src/primitive_adoption_schema.rs\n- crates/franken-engine/tests/frx_primitive_adoption_schema.rs\n- docs/FRX_PRIMITIVE_ADOPTION_SCHEMA_V1.md\n- docs/frx_primitive_adoption_schema_v1.json\n- scripts/run_frx_primitive_adoption_schema_gate.sh\n\nValidation (rch-only heavy cargo):\n- PASS: cargo check -p frankenengine-engine --test frx_primitive_adoption_schema\n- PASS: cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --test frx_primitive_adoption_schema (7/7)\n- FAIL (unrelated pre-existing compile/lint blockers outside this bead scope): cargo clippy -p frankenengine-engine --test frx_primitive_adoption_schema -- -D warnings\n  blockers observed in crates/franken-engine/src/structural_causal_model.rs (E0308 mismatched types at lines ~509/512/742/746, unused variable at ~801, pattern issue at ~1080).\n\nDeterministic gate artifacts:\n- artifacts/frx_primitive_adoption_schema/20260225T070554Z/run_manifest.json\n- artifacts/frx_primitive_adoption_schema/20260225T070554Z/events.jsonl\n- artifacts/frx_primitive_adoption_schema/20260225T070554Z/commands.txt\n\nReplay:\n- ./scripts/run_frx_primitive_adoption_schema_gate.sh ci","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:14:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.16.2","title":"[FRX-16.2] Reproducibility + Legal + Provenance Artifact Pack Automation","description":"Implement reproducibility/legal/provenance pack generator for FRX claims.

Required artifacts:
- env.json,
- manifest.json,
- repro.lock,
- LEGAL.md when risk applies,
- provenance fingerprints (toolchain/config/git).","acceptance_criteria":"1. Artifact pack generation is automated and deterministic.
2. Claim publication is blocked if required pack elements are missing/invalid.
3. Pack validation is integrated into CI/release checks.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlTower","created_at":"2026-02-25T02:39:24.665611595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:05:00.206071187Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:05:00.206043245Z","close_reason":"done: reproducibility_provenance_pack.rs — 39 tests. ToolchainFingerprint (rustc/cargo/LLVM/linker/target/edition), GitFingerprint (SHA/tree/branch/dirty/tags), BuildEnvironment (env.json model), ArtifactManifest with sorted entries + hash chain (manifest.json), DependencySnapshot with sorted entries (repro.lock), LegalAssessment with LicenseRisk (4 levels) + LicenseFinding (LEGAL.md), ReproducibilityPack with content-addressed pack_id + verify_integrity(), PackBuilder (fluent), ReproducibilityReport CI-readable with SHA-256. All tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","extreme-optimization","frankenreact-sidecar","lane-governance","phase-frontier","react-compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.16.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.16.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T02:39:30.565176339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.16.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T02:39:30.928401286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-mjh3.17","title":"[FRX-17] Information-Theoretic Observability and Witness Compression Program","description":"Program intent: turn observability, replay evidence, and proof artifacts into a mathematically budgeted channel with explicit rate-distortion and tail-risk guarantees, so the sidecar can stay auditable at scale without telemetry bloat or blind spots.

Alien-artifact scope:
- model evidence pipelines as constrained channels with bounded overhead budgets,
- derive optimal probe/feature sets under utility, latency, and memory constraints,
- compile compact witness objects that preserve replay/proof sufficiency,
- enforce fail-closed behavior when observability quality degrades below certified thresholds.

Why this matters:
Without explicit information-theoretic design, evidence systems either explode in cost or silently lose forensic power in tail events. This feature makes observability itself a first-class, provably governed subsystem.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Observability channel model defines explicit rate-distortion envelopes for evidence classes (decision, replay, optimization, security).
2. Probe selection and witness compression policies are derived from declared objective functions and resource budgets, not ad-hoc heuristics.
3. Runtime emits quality-of-observability monitors with deterministic fail-closed thresholds.
4. Reproducible artifacts demonstrate overhead reduction without degrading replay/proof sufficiency on tail incidents.

Program-Wide Test Gate:
- Every child task must define comprehensive unit-test scope, end-to-end scenario scripts, and detailed structured logging artifacts.
- Feature closure requires linked evidence that unit/e2e suites passed with deterministic fixtures and replay/triage logs.
- Any missing or stale test/logging evidence blocks milestone promotion and release gating.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"VioletReef","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:41:52.599758718Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T19:21:45.339127011Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","execution-swarm","frankenreact-sidecar","information-theory","observability","react-compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:38.841777298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:38.937842203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:39.033765093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.17.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:39.134297709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":298,"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FRX-17 checkpoint (not closing yet): added deterministic integration/gate surface for observability information-theoretic contract.

Implemented in this slice:
- docs/OBSERVABILITY_INFORMATION_THEORETIC_CHANNEL.md
- crates/franken-engine/tests/observability_channel_model.rs
- scripts/run_observability_information_theoretic_gate.sh
- README.md gate documentation section

New integration test coverage (cross-module):
- canonical channel-family + fail-closed replay lossiness checks
- multimode probe manifest determinism and incident-mode coverage monotonicity
- entropy certificate + sentinel emergency demotion (full replay capture)

Validation (heavy lanes via rch through gate script):
- ./scripts/run_observability_information_theoretic_gate.sh ci
  - PASS check + test + clippy
  - artifacts/observability_information_theoretic/20260225T103428Z/run_manifest.json
  - artifacts/observability_information_theoretic/20260225T103428Z/events.jsonl
  - artifacts/observability_information_theoretic/20260225T103428Z/commands.txt

Notes:
- rch pre-sync emits non-fatal repo_updater `--format` warnings; command outcomes remained valid.
- Remaining FRX-17 work should continue with deeper runtime adoption/overhead-evidence linkage across broader lanes.","created_at":"2026-02-25T10:46:48Z"},{"id":305,"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fresh-eyes audit checkpoint: reviewed FRX-17 files + parser compatibility contract lane with new eyes. Fixed concrete contract gap in crates/franken-engine/tests/parser_api_compatibility_contract.rs by (1) honoring PARSER_API_COMPAT_CASE filtering in compatibility_vectors_are_deterministic_and_meet_slos, and (2) enforcing required error_code key presence for failing vectors when contract keys demand it. rch validations: PASS - rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/var/tmp/rch_target_franken_engine_parser_api_compatibility PARSER_API_COMPAT_CASE=script_rejects_export cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --test parser_api_compatibility_contract compatibility_vectors_are_deterministic_and_meet_slos -- --exact --nocapture ; PASS - rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/var/tmp/rch_target_franken_engine_observability_information_theoretic cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --test observability_channel_model.","created_at":"2026-02-25T19:21:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.17.1","title":"[FRX-17.1] Observability Channel Model and Rate-Distortion Constitution","description":"Define a formal channel model for evidence generation and transport across compiler/runtime/control-plane paths.

Execution scope:
- classify evidence payload families (decision, replay, optimization, security, legal provenance),
- define utility functions and distortion metrics per payload family,
- derive explicit rate-distortion envelopes and failure budgets,
- publish a constitutional policy that forbids uncapped telemetry or unverifiable lossy compression.

Alien-artifact artifacts:
- rate-distortion frontier tables,
- distortion-to-risk conversion ledger,
- machine-readable policy schema consumed by runtime and CI gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Evidence families have explicit utility/distortion definitions and measurable telemetry fields.
2. Rate-distortion frontier is derived for each family with hard budget thresholds.
3. Distortion levels are mapped to operational risk categories and fallback triggers.
4. Policy schema is machine-readable and referenced by runtime/CI gates.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlTower","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:42:30.998287742Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:35:52.003280025Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:35:52.003253406Z","close_reason":"done: observability_channel_model.rs implemented with 40 tests — PayloadFamily (5), DistortionMetric (5), ChannelPath (5), RateDistortionEnvelope with interpolation, FailureBudget with fail-closed, ChannelSpec, ChannelState with emit/drop/drain/violation tracking, ChannelReport CI-readable with SHA-256, canonical_channel_specs (5 channels), canonical_risk_ledgers, constitutional constraints (no uncapped telemetry, no unverifiable lossy, no backpressure overflow). All 40 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenreact-sidecar","information-theory","observability","proof-carrying"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:45:25.081733488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:45:24.900263440Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.17.2","title":"[FRX-17.2] Optimal Probe Design and Budgeted Observability Allocation","description":"Design probe selection as a constrained optimization problem (submodular coverage + convex resource allocation) rather than ad-hoc instrumentation.

Execution scope:
- define candidate probe universe across compiler/runtime/router/evidence pipeline,
- optimize probe sets for maximal forensic utility under latency/memory overhead caps,
- emit deterministic probe schedules for normal, degraded, and incident modes,
- integrate per-mode observability budgets into one-lever optimization governance.

Alien-artifact artifacts:
- probe utility ledger + marginal gain traces,
- approximation/optimality certificates,
- mode-specific probe manifests with budget proofs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Probe universe and utility functions are documented and reproducible.
2. Optimization output provides either approximation guarantees or primal-dual optimality evidence.
3. Mode-specific probe manifests satisfy latency/memory caps in benchmark fixtures.
4. Regression gates fail when probe budgets or observability utility constraints are violated.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlTower","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:42:59.821272214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T09:20:03.578376679Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T09:20:03.578352865Z","close_reason":"done: observability_probe_design.rs — 41 tests, greedy submodular probe selection, multi-mode schedules, approximation certificates, utility ledger","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["convex","frankenreact-sidecar","observability","optimization","submodular"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:45:25.259698068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.17.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:45:25.443180654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -764,7 +764,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.18.1","title":"[FRX-18.1] Attack-Surface Game Model and Asymmetric Loss Formalization","description":"Model compiler/runtime/control-plane attack-defense interactions as sequential games with explicit asymmetric losses and action constraints.

Execution scope:
- enumerate strategic attacker and defender action spaces by subsystem,
- define payoff/loss tensors including user harm, performance cost, and false-positive containment cost,
- encode admissible defense actions and hard constraints (safe-mode guarantees, deterministic replay obligations),
- publish machine-readable game model artifacts for simulation and policy synthesis.

Alien-artifact artifacts:
- game specification schemas,
- loss tensor registry,
- admissible-action automata.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Strategic action spaces and constraints are complete for declared threat classes.
2. Loss/payoff tensors are explicitly documented and tied to measurable runtime outcomes.
3. Admissible defense-action set preserves deterministic replay and constitutional safety invariants.
4. Game model is machine-readable and consumed by downstream synthesis/simulation tasks.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.
- Add end-to-end test scripts that exercise real user workflows plus adversarial/degraded scenarios, including fallback and recovery paths.
- Emit detailed structured logs for unit and e2e runs (scenario_id, trace_id when available, seed, timing, decision path, outcome) and retain artifacts for replay/triage.
- CI must fail on unit/e2e regression or missing logging artifacts for this bead's deliverables.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlTower","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:43:32.616934585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:37:18.438629477Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:37:18.438602577Z","close_reason":"done: attack_surface_game_model.rs — 27 tests, 0 failures. Subsystem/Player/ActionSpace/LossTensor with minimax, AdmissibleActionAutomaton, GameModel, GameModelReport","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["control-plane","decision-theory","frankenreact-sidecar","game-theory","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.18.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:45:26.734901612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.18.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:45:26.915213373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mjh3.18.2","title":"[FRX-18.2] Adversarial Coevolution Harness with Regret-Bounded Policy Search","description":"Build a continuous attacker-defender simulation harness that discovers exploit classes and compiles defense updates under no-regret/bounded-regret learning guarantees.

Execution scope:
- implement deterministic tournament harness over representative workload and threat fixtures,
- run adversarial policy search with bounded exploration budgets,
- generate exploit/defense trajectory ledgers and convergence diagnostics,
- emit candidate defense policy deltas with replay and regression evidence.

Alien-artifact artifacts:
- regret budget dashboard,
- exploit-class emergence timelines,
- policy-delta packs ready for gate evaluation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Tournament harness is deterministic and replayable across seeds, fixtures, and policy versions.
2. Policy search reports bounded-regret or explicit convergence diagnostics under declared assumptions.
3. Exploit trajectories are stored with minimization artifacts and class taxonomy.
4. Candidate defense deltas include non-regression evidence against compatibility and latency gates.

Testing and Logging Contract:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for primary, edge, and failure paths with deterministic fixtures and explicit expected outcomes.

 {"id":"bd-mjh3.19","title":"[FRX-19] Semantic Twin, Counterfactual Replay, and Bifurcation Governance Program","description":"Program intent: build an executable semantic twin of FrankenReact sidecar behavior that supports counterfactual reasoning, regime-shift early warning, and automated rollback policy synthesis before production harm occurs.

Alien-artifact scope:
- compile causal/structural models linking route decisions, fallbacks, workload characteristics, and user-visible outcomes,
- run counterfactual replay to evaluate alternate policy decisions against observed incidents,
- detect stability boundary crossings and bifurcation risk as parameters drift,
- synthesize deterministic rollback or safe-mode transitions with explicit assumptions and proof obligations.

Why this matters:
Deterministic replay tells us what happened. Counterfactual twin analysis tells us what should have happened and how to avoid repeating costly decisions.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Semantic twin model links runtime decisions to outcome metrics with explicit, testable assumptions.
2. Counterfactual replay pipeline can evaluate alternate decisions on real incident traces deterministically.
3. Bifurcation/stability monitors identify parameter regions requiring preemptive demotion or safe-mode routing.
4. Rollback/safe-mode synthesis emits deterministic policy patches with proof obligations and replay-backed evidence.

Program-Wide Test Gate:
- Every child task must define comprehensive unit-test scope, end-to-end scenario scripts, and detailed structured logging artifacts.
- Feature closure requires linked evidence that unit/e2e suites passed with deterministic fixtures and replay/triage logs.
- Any missing or stale test/logging evidence blocks milestone promotion and release gating.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-25T03:42:16.648426587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:34:08.697129928Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","causal-inference","dynamical-systems","frankenreact-sidecar","governance","react-compiler","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.19","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:39.832336284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.19","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.19.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:39.931942574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.19","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.19.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:40.032114549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mjh3.19","depends_on_id":"bd-mjh3.19.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:25:40.131659134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
